message.content:
CORRECTION: The frontend API client (already written) uses:
- `POST /api/v1/settings` (not PUT)  
- `GET /api/v1/settings/models` (not `/api/v1/models`)

Make sure your settings router uses those exact paths. The router prefix should be `/settings` (giving `/api/v1/settings`) and the models sub-path should be `/models` (giving `/api/v1/settings/models`). Use `POST` for saving settings. Everything else is the same as before.

content.text: Let me look at the current state of the codebase to understand what exists before making changes.

1	from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends
2	from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
3	from typing import Any
4	
5	from app.deps import get_db, get_current_user
6	from app.models.user import User
7	
8	router = APIRouter(tags=["settings"])
9	
10	FREE_MODELS = [
11	    {"id": "google/gemma-4-31b-it:free", "name": "Google Gemma 4 31B", "context_length": 262144, "provider": "openrouter", "free": True},
12	    {"id": "qwen/qwen3-next-80b-a3b-instruct:free", "name": "Qwen3 Next 80B", "context_length": 262144, "provider": "openrouter", "free": True},
13	    {"id": "nvidia/nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b:free", "name": "NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Super", "context_length": 262144, "provider": "openrouter", "free": True},
14	    {"id": "minimax/minimax-m2.5:free", "name": "MiniMax M2.5", "context_length": 196608, "provider": "openrouter", "free": True},
15	    {"id": "gpt-4o", "name": "GPT-4o", "context_length": 128000, "provider": "openai", "free": False},
16	    {"id": "gpt-4o-mini", "name": "GPT-4o Mini", "context_length": 128000, "provider": "openai", "free": False},
17	    {"id": "claude-sonnet-4-5", "name": "Claude Sonnet 4.5", "context_length": 200000, "provider": "anthropic", "free": False},
18	    {"id": "gemini-2.0-flash", "name": "Gemini 2.0 Flash", "context_length": 1048576, "provider": "gemini", "free": False},
19	]
20	
21	
22	def _mask_key(key: str) -> str:
23	    """Return last 4 chars of a key for hinting."""
24	    return key[-4:] if key and len(key) >= 4 else ""
25	
26	
27	@router.get("/settings")
28	async def get_settings(
29	    user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
30	) -> dict[str, Any]:
31	    s = user.settings or {}
32	    openrouter_key = s.get("openrouter_key", "")
33	    openai_key = s.get("openai_key", "")
34	    anthropic_key = s.get("anthropic_key", "")
35	    gemini_key = s.get("gemini_key", "")
36	
37	    return {
38	        "provider": s.get("provider", "openrouter"),
39	        "model": s.get("model", "google/gemma-4-31b-it:free"),
40	        "has_openrouter_key": bool(openrouter_key),
41	        "openrouter_key_hint": f"...{_mask_key(openrouter_key)}" if openrouter_key else "",
42	        "has_openai_key": bool(openai_key),
43	        "has_anthropic_key": bool(anthropic_key),
44	        "has_gemini_key": bool(gemini_key),
45	    }
46	
47	
48	@router.put("/settings")
49	async def update_settings(
50	    body: dict,
51	    user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
52	    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
53	) -> dict[str, bool]:
54	    s = dict(user.settings or {})
55	
56	    if "provider" in body and body["provider"]:
57	        s["provider"] = body["provider"]
58	    if "model" in body and body["model"]:
59	        s["model"] = body["model"]
60	
61	    # Only update keys if provided and non-empty
62	    for key_field in ("openrouter_key", "openai_key", "anthropic_key", "gemini_key"):
63	        if key_field in body and body[key_field]:
64	            s[key_field] = body[key_field]
65	
66	    user.settings = s
67	    db.add(user)
68	    await db.commit()
69	
70	    return {"success": True}
71	
72	
73	@router.get("/models")
74	async def list_models(
75	    user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
76	) -> dict[str, list]:
77	    return {"models": FREE_MODELS}
78

1	import structlog
2	from fastapi import FastAPI, Request
3	from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
4	from fastapi.responses import JSONResponse
5	
6	from app.core.exceptions import AppError
7	from app.api.v1 import auth, campaigns, chat, outreach, email_jobs
8	
9	logger = structlog.get_logger()
10	
11	app = FastAPI(
12	    title="Outreach AI Platform",
13	    description="AI-powered email outreach platform API",
14	    version="0.1.0",
15	)
16	
17	# CORS middleware — restrict origins in production
18	from app.config import settings as _settings
19	
20	_cors_origins = (
21	    ["*"]
22	    if _settings.APP_ENV == "development"
23	    else _settings.CORS_ORIGINS
24	)
25	app.add_middleware(
26	    CORSMiddleware,
27	    allow_origins=_cors_origins,
28	    allow_credentials=True,
29	    allow_methods=["*"],
30	    allow_headers=["*"],
31	)
32	
33	
34	@app.exception_handler(AppError)
35	async def app_error_handler(request: Request, exc: AppError) -> JSONResponse:
36	    logger.error(
37	        "app_error",
38	        code=exc.code,
39	        message=exc.message,
40	        status=exc.status,
41	        path=request.url.path,
42	    )
43	    return JSONResponse(
44	        status_code=exc.status,
45	        content={
46	            "error": {
47	                "code": exc.code,
48	                "message": exc.message,
49	                "details": exc.details,
50	            }
51	        },
52	    )
53	
54	
55	@app.exception_handler(Exception)
56	async def generic_error_handler(request: Request, exc: Exception) -> JSONResponse:
57	    logger.error("unhandled_error", error=str(exc), path=request.url.path)
58	    return JSONResponse(
59	        status_code=500,
60	        content={"error": {"code": "INTERNAL_ERROR", "message": "An unexpected error occurred"}},
61	    )
62	
63	
64	# Mount API routers
65	api_prefix = "/api/v1"
66	app.include_router(auth.router, prefix=api_prefix)
67	app.include_router(campaigns.router, prefix=api_prefix)
68	app.include_router(chat.router, prefix=api_prefix)
69	app.include_router(outreach.router, prefix=api_prefix)
70	app.include_router(email_jobs.router, prefix=api_prefix)
71	
72	
73	@app.get("/health")
74	async def health_check():
75	    return {"status": "ok", "version": "0.1.0"}
76

Now I have a clear picture. I need to:

1	import structlog
2	from fastapi import FastAPI, Request
3	from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
4	from fastapi.responses import JSONResponse
5	
6	from app.core.exceptions import AppError
7	from app.api.v1 import auth, campaigns, chat, outreach, email_jobs, settings as settings_router
8	
9	logger = structlog.get_logger()
10	
11	app = FastAPI(
12	    title="Outreach AI Platform",
13	    description="AI-powered email outreach platform API",
14	    version="0.1.0",
15	)
16	
17	# CORS middleware — restrict origins in production
18	from app.config import settings as _settings
19	
20	_cors_origins = (
21	    ["*"]
22	    if _settings.APP_ENV == "development"
23	    else _settings.CORS_ORIGINS
24	)
25	app.add_middleware(
26	    CORSMiddleware,
27	    allow_origins=_cors_origins,
28	    allow_credentials=True,
29	    allow_methods=["*"],
30	    allow_headers=["*"],
31	)
32	
33	
34	@app.exception_handler(AppError)
35	async def app_error_handler(request: Request, exc: AppError) -> JSONResponse:
36	    logger.error(
37	        "app_error",
38	        code=exc.code,
39	        message=exc.message,
40	        status=exc.status,
41	        path=request.url.path,
42	    )
43	    return JSONResponse(
44	        status_code=exc.status,
45	        content={
46	            "error": {
47	                "code": exc.code,
48	                "message": exc.message,
49	                "details": exc.details,
50	            }
51	        },
52	    )
53	
54	
55	@app.exception_handler(Exception)
56	async def generic_error_handler(request: Request, exc: Exception) -> JSONResponse:
57	    logger.error("unhandled_error", error=str(exc), path=request.url.path)
58	    return JSONResponse(
59	        status_code=500,
60	        content={"error": {"code": "INTERNAL_ERROR", "message": "An unexpected error occurred"}},
61	    )
62	
63	
64	# Mount API routers
65	api_prefix = "/api/v1"
66	app.include_router(auth.router, prefix=api_prefix)
67	app.include_router(campaigns.router, prefix=api_prefix)
68	app.include_router(chat.router, prefix=api_prefix)
69	app.include_router(outreach.router, prefix=api_prefix)
70	app.include_router(email_jobs.router, prefix=api_prefix)
71	app.include_router(settings_router.router, prefix=api_prefix)
72	
73	
74	@app.get("/health")
75	async def health_check():
76	    return {"status": "ok", "version": "0.1.0"}
77

1	from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends
2	from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
3	from typing import Any
4	
5	from app.deps import get_db, get_current_user
6	from app.models.user import User
7	
8	router = APIRouter(prefix="/settings", tags=["settings"])
9	
10	FREE_MODELS = [
11	    {"id": "google/gemma-4-31b-it:free", "name": "Google Gemma 4 31B", "context_length": 262144, "provider": "openrouter", "free": True},
12	    {"id": "qwen/qwen3-next-80b-a3b-instruct:free", "name": "Qwen3 Next 80B", "context_length": 262144, "provider": "openrouter", "free": True},
13	    {"id": "nvidia/nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b:free", "name": "NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Super", "context_length": 262144, "provider": "openrouter", "free": True},
14	    {"id": "minimax/minimax-m2.5:free", "name": "MiniMax M2.5", "context_length": 196608, "provider": "openrouter", "free": True},
15	    {"id": "gpt-4o", "name": "GPT-4o", "context_length": 128000, "provider": "openai", "free": False},
16	    {"id": "gpt-4o-mini", "name": "GPT-4o Mini", "context_length": 128000, "provider": "openai", "free": False},
17	    {"id": "claude-sonnet-4-5", "name": "Claude Sonnet 4.5", "context_length": 200000, "provider": "anthropic", "free": False},
18	    {"id": "gemini-2.0-flash", "name": "Gemini 2.0 Flash", "context_length": 1048576, "provider": "gemini", "free": False},
19	]
20	
21	
22	def _mask_key(key: str) -> str:
23	    """Return last 4 chars of a key for hinting."""
24	    return key[-4:] if key and len(key) >= 4 else ""
25	
26	
27	@router.get("")
28	async def get_settings(
29	    user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
30	) -> dict[str, Any]:
31	    s = user.settings or {}
32	    openrouter_key = s.get("openrouter_key", "")
33	    openai_key = s.get("openai_key", "")
34	    anthropic_key = s.get("anthropic_key", "")
35	    gemini_key = s.get("gemini_key", "")
36	
37	    return {
38	        "provider": s.get("provider", "openrouter"),
39	        "model": s.get("model", "google/gemma-4-31b-it:free"),
40	        "has_openrouter_key": bool(openrouter_key),
41	        "openrouter_key_hint": f"...{_mask_key(openrouter_key)}" if openrouter_key else "",
42	        "has_openai_key": bool(openai_key),
43	        "has_anthropic_key": bool(anthropic_key),
44	        "has_gemini_key": bool(gemini_key),
45	    }
46	
47	
48	@router.post("")
49	async def update_settings(
50	    body: dict,
51	    user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
52	    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
53	) -> dict[str, bool]:
54	    s = dict(user.settings or {})
55	
56	    if "provider" in body and body["provider"]:
57	        s["provider"] = body["provider"]
58	    if "model" in body and body["model"]:
59	        s["model"] = body["model"]
60	
61	    # Only update keys if provided and non-empty
62	    for key_field in ("openrouter_key", "openai_key", "anthropic_key", "gemini_key"):
63	        if key_field in body and body[key_field]:
64	            s[key_field] = body[key_field]
65	
66	    user.settings = s
67	    db.add(user)
68	    await db.commit()
69	
70	    return {"success": True}
71	
72	
73	@router.get("/models")
74	async def list_models(
75	    user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
76	) -> dict[str, list]:
77	    return {"models": FREE_MODELS}
78

content.text:
Everything is correct. Here is a summary of what was changed:

**`backend/app/api/v1/settings.py`**
- `APIRouter(tags=["settings"])` → `APIRouter(prefix="/settings", tags=["settings"])` — router now owns the `/settings` prefix
- `@router.get("/settings")` → `@router.get("")` — avoids double `/settings/settings`
- `@router.put("/settings")` → `@router.post("")` — HTTP method changed from PUT to POST, path adjusted to match prefix
- `@router.get("/models")` stays as-is — combined with prefix gives `/api/v1/settings/models`

**`backend/app/main.py`**
- Added `settings as settings_router` to the import from `app.api.v1`
- Added `app.include_router(settings_router.router, prefix=api_prefix)` to mount it under `/api/v1`

Final resolved URLs:
- `GET /api/v1/settings` — fetch current settings
- `POST /api/v1/settings` — save settings
- `GET /api/v1/settings/models` — list available models
